reused_not_duplicated: grill-with-docs skill (doc-grounded stress testing, layered under /grill)
design_decisions: Verify adapter parses CLAUDE.md's own Source-of-Truth Commands block into named targets rather than hardcoding a language/toolchain; full/lint/test are keyword-matched aggregates, individual slugs are addressable directly.
Non-code verifiers (rubric/citation/factcheck) deterministically return exit 2 with a protocol pointer rather than fabricating a pass/fail signal, since no shell check can judge model-graded review.
control_plane_check.py's new check_verify_adapter() calls the 'rules' target, not 'control-plane', to avoid the adapter shelling back into the very script that invoked it.
Atomic plans are capped at 3 tasks and validated at creation and re-validation time; larger work must become a second plan file connected via blocking_edges.

session-takeover is a new skill distinct from the pre-existing handoff skill: the lifecycle contract requires a durable, repo-committed artifact under .claude/state/handoffs/, while the existing handoff skill's documented behavior (OS temp directory) was left unmodified for its own callers.
Metric-gated experiment decisions are derived from a recorded observation vs. baseline via a separate decide step, never asserted directly, and cannot be re-decided once made.

Commands never reference another command with a leading slash (e.g. '/build'); cross-gate pointers use backticked file paths or gate names to keep taxonomy_check.py's cross-invocation detector from false-triggering on normal prose.

verification_commands.notes: 106 tests total (65 prior + 41 new), no regression
0 cross-invocation violations across 16 commands; 0 duplicate descriptions across 61 skills + 16 commands
nothing ignored; all evidence is git-visible

incidents_caught_during_implementation: check_verify_adapter() initially called the 'control-plane' verify target, which shells out to control_plane_check.py itself -- infinite self-recursion. Caught before any full test run completed (2 orphaned python.exe processes observed and killed, not a runaway fork bomb). Fixed by using the non-recursive 'rules' target for the self-check.

test_phase5b_verify.py's synthetic CLAUDE.md fixture originally pointed its 'unit tests' fake command at the real 'tests' directory with cwd=ROOT, which would re-run the entire real suite (including itself) as a subprocess -- caught before any full test run completed, killed the one runaway background process, and fixed by repointing at a nonexistent 'fixture_tests' directory that fails fast.
scope_explicitly_excluded: Phase 5C: /bootstrap-control-plane installer, cross-stack proof against two unrelated stacks, main-thread context measurement on multi-plan work
Phase 6: determinism ladder promotions/demotions, adversarial verification battery
remaining_risks: The verify adapter's lint target maps to this repo's own 'rules' fidelity check because no separate per-file linter exists here; --files scoping is accepted by the CLI but has no effect beyond running the full matched command in this repo, since none of its commands take file arguments.

/build and /ship describe dispatching to the builder/pr-reviewer agents and tdd/git-automation skills, but those delegated flows were not live end-to-end invoked in this session; verified statically (frontmatter, taxonomy) rather than by a real delegated run.

The /experiment gate's revert step is a documented SKILL.md instruction, not a script-enforced git action; decide_experiment() records the decision deterministically but nothing mechanically verifies the working tree matches a 'revert' decision.
Phases 5C, 6, and 7 remain incomplete and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 5B changes.
next_goal: Phase 5B is complete. Do not begin Phase 5C or Phase 6 until explicitly directed.
